_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize MI federal-House challengers (MI-08 Smith; MI-10 open-seat primary: Jaye, Hines, Chung, Lulgjuraj). Social-media protocol — no congressional record. Candidacy currency verified: ALL still active for the Aug 4 2026 primary. Cross-party catch: Chung (D) explicitly backs E-Verify.

thomas-j-smith-mi-08.notes_set: Thomas J. Smith — listed as a declared Republican candidate in the Aug 4 2026 MI-08 primary (challenging Rep. Kristen McDonald Rivet) per Ballotpedia/Politics1, but with essentially zero public campaign footprint: no campaign website, no traceable social media, no Vote411 answers, no issue-group endorsements, and no confirmed active FEC committee surfaced. Some candidate listings omit him entirely. Confidence held at roster_verified — candidacy confirmed but ZERO policy positions findable; nothing scored rather than fabricate. Re-check status near the filing deadline.

profile.background: Attorney and small-business owner (formerly McGuireWoods, D.C.); Oakland County Democratic Party Executive Committee; repeat congressional candidate (ran MI-09 2022). FEC H2MI08154.

brian-steven-jaye.notes_set: Brian Steven Jaye — Democrat, MI-10 open-seat primary (Aug 4 2026), ACTIVE (campaign site + Ballotpedia primary list). Identity confirmed: the attorney/repeat candidate (prev. MI-09 2022, lost), NOT a county commissioner; the 'veteran' claim in one search summary is uncorroborated. Documented progressive across the board: pro-choice (VoteProChoice endorsement), pro-LGBTQ (MI Dem Party LGBTQ+ Caucus endorsement), opposes school vouchers (NEA/MEA endorsed), gun-control 'Democratic Gun Sense Candidate' (universal background checks, safe storage). No cross-party deviations. Religion not stated. Many cells null — no public position found.

0.note: Pro-choice; endorsed by VoteProChoice and campaigns to 'protect the rights of women' — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Pro-abortion-rights; no advocacy for restrictions, let alone abolition.

0.note: Endorsed by MI Dem Party LGBTQ+ Caucus; vows LGBTQ should 'have the same liberties as every American' — does not affirm marriage as exclusively man-woman.

1.note: Pro-LGBTQ-rights posture; does not oppose same-sex marriage/civil unions.

4.note: Vows to 'defend the LGBTQ+ community from discrimination' — supports, not opposes, LGBTQ identity in policy.

0.note: Opposes school vouchers; 'strongly fund and support public education'; endorsed by NEA and Michigan Education Association.
Self-described 'National Democratic Gun Sense Candidate' backing universal background checks — opposite of permitless constitutional carry.

1.note: Backs universal background checks and 'safe storage' mandates — supports the gun-control measures this cell opposes.

2.note: Gun-control candidate — does not support repealing the NFA/GCA.

profile.background: Career prosecutor (Wayne Co. assistant prosecutor; Washtenaw Co. Special Victims Unit); 2024 Democratic nominee for Macomb County Prosecutor (lost to Peter Lucido). FEC H6MI10276.

christina-hines.notes_set: Christina Hines — Democrat, MI-10 open-seat primary (Aug 4 2026), ACTIVE. Former prosecutor; lost the 2024 Macomb Co. Prosecutor race, now a first-time U.S. House candidate. Pro-abortion-rights (EMILY's List endorsement) and gun-control (GIFFORDS endorsement: universal background checks, red-flag laws, safe storage). As prosecutor expanded Fair Michigan to prosecute anti-LGBTQ hate crimes. CROSS-PARTY NOTE: her platform calls for 'increased border security' and 'stricter penalties for fentanyl' — enforcement-leaning language for a Democrat, but too non-specific to score the wall/deport cells (logged as a deviation in notes, cells null). Religion not stated.

0.note: Endorsed by EMILY's List (endorses only pro-abortion-rights Democratic women); runs to 'protect reproductive rights' — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Pro-abortion-rights; no advocacy for restrictions or abolition.

4.note: EMILY's List endorsee — accepts the abortion-industry bundling/funding network this cell asks candidates to have refused.
As prosecutor expanded the Fair Michigan program to prosecute anti-LGBTQ hate crimes — affirmative pro-LGBTQ record, opposite of opposing LGBTQ promotion in policy.

0.note: GIFFORDS-endorsed; backs universal background checks — opposite of permitless constitutional carry.

1.note: Explicitly supports red-flag laws and strengthened safe-storage requirements — the gun-control measures this cell opposes.

2.note: Calls gun violence a 'public health issue' and would prioritize stronger gun laws — does not support repealing the NFA/GCA.

profile.background: Constitutional attorney; ex-DOC lawyer (CHIPS & Science Act) and ex-DOJ Civil Rights Division; advised Senate Judiciary; former civics teacher. Son of Vietnamese refugees. Harvard College/Harvard Law. FEC H6MI10292; fundraising frontrunner.

eric-chung-mi-10.notes_set: Eric Chung — Democrat, MI-10 open-seat primary (Aug 4 2026), ACTIVE fundraising frontrunner ($1.46M raised). First-time candidate; would be Michigan's first openly LGBTQ member of Congress (LGBTQ Victory Fund + Equality PAC endorsed). Pro-choice ('restore Roe'). CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION (HARD): explicitly supports a working E-Verify system — scored TRUE on border q3. Also backs path-to-citizenship (so opposes mandatory deportation of all — q1 FALSE) and 'focus enforcement on violent criminals.' No evidence on guns, elections, religious liberty, monetary policy, K-12 culture issues. Religion not stated.

0.note: Campaigns to 'Restore the protections of Roe v. Wade' — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Pro-abortion-rights (restore Roe); no advocacy for restrictions or abolition.

0.note: Openly LGBTQ candidate endorsed by LGBTQ Victory Fund and Equality PAC; would be MI's first openly LGBTQ member of Congress — does not affirm marriage as exclusively man-woman.

1.note: LGBTQ Victory Fund/Equality PAC endorsee — does not oppose same-sex marriage.

4.note: Pledges every Michigander 'including LGBTQ+ people' feels safe to live and prosper — supports, not opposes, LGBTQ identity in policy.

1.note: Backs 'comprehensive immigration reform that... creates a path to citizenship' and focusing enforcement on violent criminals — opposite of mandatory deportation of all illegal aliens.

3.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: explicitly supports E-Verify — 'Ensure a working E-Verify system, so that employers can have confidence in hiring.'

profile.background: Former assistant prosecutor, Macomb County (prior Wayne County trial attorney); grandson of Albanian immigrants who fled communism; Holy Cross College/U. Detroit Mercy Law (Federalist Society president); Youth Chair, Macomb County GOP. FEC H6MI10326.

robert-lulgjuraj.notes_set: Robert Lulgjuraj — Republican, MI-10 open-seat primary (Aug 4 2026), ACTIVE: survived two May 2026 ballot-eligibility challenges; the MI Board of State Canvassers kept him on the ballot (May 21 2026). Competitive GOP primary vs. Michael Bouchard. Documented conservative platform: finish the border wall + oppose amnesty; voter photo ID + proof-of-citizenship and tightly limited absentee voting; Balanced Budget Amendment; 'no more endless wars.' Platform is SILENT on the life/marriage/LGBTQ/guns axis — no Right to Life/SBA or NRA endorsement found — so those cells are null (NOT inferred from his party or Catholic faith). Religion: Catholic.

2.note: Platform supports a Balanced Budget Amendment and keeping taxes low while cutting 'waste, fraud, and abuse.'

1.note: Supports voter photo ID and proof of citizenship to register.

2.note: Would limit absentee ballots to deployed military and mobility-limited seniors — i.e. in-person voting with narrow verified exceptions.

3.note: Restricting absentee voting to narrow exceptions opposes mass mail-in voting.

0.note: 'Finish the border wall' and support Border Patrol/ICE.

1.note: 'Deport illegal immigrants starting with dangerous criminals' and 'always oppose amnesty.'
'No more endless, senseless wars that cost American lives and drain our treasury'; deploy the military only when vital national interests are at risk.
